highlights.text: I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend, until it ends.
My lean is we work back up to 6050, then 6081.

For two weeks, ES had been stuck inside the same range, which I’ve defined daily as being 5978 support (we set big lows here on June 5th, June 12th, then put in 10 point Failed Breakdowns on June 19th and this Sunday), with resistance being up at 6081.
To trade, we need an entry setup and as readers know, mine is the Failed Breakdown.
The past two weeks have seen a marriage between this triangle pattern, my core setup The Failed Breakdown, and headline flow originating from the Middle East conflict.
The cycle is generally the same: Bearish headline causes price to flush, it loses a major low, puts in a Failed Breakdown, recovers.

The chart pattern enters the picture here, because it tells us where the Failed Breakdowns are likely to occur and for two weeks, most my entries have occurred on Failed Breakdowns of 5978 triangle support.
” I also wrote on Friday at 4pm: “…Wait for a Failed Breakdown of Thursdays 5970 low.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into some of the Failed Breakdowns we had at the 5978 zone over the last week, with particular emphasis on the one we saw last Thursday where we flushed that zone, trapped shorts, rallied 100 points into Friday.
Inside this range, 6050 has been the mid-point support or what I call a magnet.
5978-82 to 6081= a range (descending triangle) with 6050 a key magnet inside.

post_title: SPX Finally Breaks Out Its 2 Week Range. Where To From Here? June 25 Plan
